findings.evidence: The overwrite reveal is followed by polished moral accounting instead of sustained embodied consequence: 'He had killed a man with jargon' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:287, 'He had deleted Dominic so completely...' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:291, and a final restatement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:313.
findings.problem: This is a moral-safety-valve form of sanitization. Once Sarah is touching him and Dominic is confirmed gone, the chapter should hold the reader inside the bodily and social wrongness of the theft. Instead, the narration becomes lucidly self-interpreting and partially does the reader's ethical work for them. The style bible explicitly requires guilt to live in Nate's body, not in tidy reflections.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the ending passage from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:281 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:315 so the pressure stays in touch, breath, panic, and Sarah's misrecognition before any thesis-level language arrives. Keep the must-land beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:285, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:299, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:307, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:315, but cut or roughen the explanatory summary centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:287 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:291 so the scene advances by immediate consequence rather than polished self-indictment.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:281 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:315 still makes clear that Nate has overwritten Dominic, Sarah is beside him calling him Dom, and he is trapped on July 11, 2005, but the reader feels those facts through on-page bodily or social pressure before the narration delivers any abstract moral diagnosis. Fail if the reveal again resolves first into thesis language about murder or deletion.

findings.evidence: The activation warnings stay vague at the moment they need to preload the actual failure mode: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:153, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:155, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:191, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:205 only become fully legible after Nate explains the overwrite at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:271 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:283.
findings.problem: The chapter's major turn depends on a specific process outcome: occupied-vector targeting resolves as consciousness overwrite rather than failed transport, collision, or simple material displacement. The page gets there eventually, but too much of that logic arrives after the fact. The result is locally repairable accidental obscurity in the chapter's most important speculative beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Sharpen the setup around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:159 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:189 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:205 so at least one warning, interface phrase, or Nate paraphrase explicitly points to identity-resolution risk when the target coordinates are already occupied. Nate can still ignore or misunderstand it, but the text should preload the exact category of failure that pays off at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:271 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:283.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, before the blackout at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:207, a cold reader can plausibly state that an occupied target may map Nate onto the occupant rather than place a second body in the room. Fail if that specific failure mode only becomes legible once Nate explains it after waking.

findings.evidence: With only one human-spoken line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:299, the chapter's quoted-speech burden falls on assistant and interface text at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:85, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:191, which repeatedly use the same calm-warning cadence.
findings.problem: The chapter does not earn a pass-level dialogue verdict because its quoted speech is dominated by one repeated ask-warning-ignore ladder. That cadence becomes predictable instead of escalating. Since there is almost no human speech here, the assistant/UI language has to carry more tonal and structural variation than it currently does.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the quoted exchanges around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:59 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:101 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:113, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:191 so they do not all sound like the same polished caution banner in different lengths. Strategy options: make one response terser and procedural, one overtechnical enough that Nate has to paraphrase it, and one misleadingly practical rather than ominous; shift more of the escalation into Nate's question phrasing and reaction beats instead of stacking similar warning copy.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each quoted assistant or interface intervention in the revised spans around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:149 changes the interaction mode or stakes rather than re-proving the same cautionary rhythm. Fail if three or more of those beats still read as interchangeable calm warnings.

findings.evidence: The chapter repeatedly lands an image and then follows it with the same interpretive construction: 'That was the first hook' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:57, 'That was almost a relief' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:81, 'That answer did more damage...' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:87, 'That was the ugliest part, maybe' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:135, 'That was what his nervous system did...' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:141, and 'That was the first thing' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:217.
findings.problem: This recurring sentence-shape starts to flatten the prose by having the narration explain beats the surrounding image or action has already made legible. The chapter is otherwise strong at physical specificity; these repeated tags sound more like autopilot interpretation than necessity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target the repeated interpretive tags at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:57, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:135, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:217 first. In those local spots, either cut the follow-up gloss entirely or replace it with image/action/sensation specific to the beat already on the page. Keep the hook, relief, ugliness, and disorientation, but stop announcing them in the same sentence formula.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:57, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:135, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:217 preserve the same narrative meaning without relying on three or more repeated 'That was...' style follow-up glosses. Fail if the chapter still uses that explanatory construction as a recurring landing mechanism across those beats.

summary: Strong premise delivery, close-third control, and sensory specificity make this an effective opener, but it still fails this review pass. The overwrite logic is not quite preloaded enough, the ending turns bodily consequence into polished moral summary at the point of maximum pressure, the quoted assistant/UI speech repeats one cautionary dialogue template, and a recurring interpretive sentence formula starts to flatten the prose.
